title: exec redirect-only permanence (exec >file / 3>file / 2>&1 persist)
description:
Last path-2 piece (M5). exec with only redirects (no command word) must apply them permanently to the shell IO table for the rest of the session.

Root cause already diagnosed: Executor#with_redirects ensure closes the file opened over base (io.ios - base.ios), undoing exec's replace_io. The dup case (exec 2>&1) already works because no new stream is opened.

Fix: skip the close when the executor committed the io (io.equal?(@io) after replace_io). Verify bit-exact vs dash: exec >f / 3>f / 2>&1 / 9<f persist; subshell isolation (( exec >f ); after) does not leak; bad-path exec already fatal (7aj). One slice, green rake.
close_reason: exec redirect-only permanence: with_redirects skips closing the io exec committed via replace_io (io.equal?(@io)); verified vs dash + corpus + unit test; green rake, 100% cov

title: function-call redirects not applied to the function body
description: `f(){ echo body; }; f >file` — dash sends body to file; rush prints body to stdout. CommandRunner#run_function does not thread the redirected io into the function body (dispatch passes io to builtin/External but run_function ignores it). Likely fix: run the function body under executor.with_io(io). Found by the 7aj redirect fuzz; orthogonal to 7aj. Verify bit-exact vs dash + add to differential corpus.
close_reason: run_function now binds the call's redirect io to the body via with_io, but only when a redirect layered a new table (io != base) so exec inside a no-redirect call still persists; verified vs dash incl. exec-scope cases + corpus + unit; green rake 100% cov

title: Phase 2 — Intermediate
close_reason: Phase 2 (Intermediate) complete: all sub-issues (.1 exec-redirect permanence, .2 function-call redirects, .3 shift abort, .4 hash/times/set -v) landed across slices 7ak-7an, each verified vs dash with green rake

title: Sorbet: inline sig {} types + srb tc in rake gate (independent from RBS/Steep)
description: Second, INDEPENDENT type system alongside RBS/Steep (rush-211.2). Per user directive: two formats for two tools, signatures deliberately NOT shared. Sorbet uses the inline sig {} DSL (extend T::Sig; sorbet-runtime dependency accepted) with # typed: sigils; 'srb tc' green in the default rake gate. Bootstrap via srb init / tapioca; raise # typed: levels gradually (false -> true -> strict). sorbet 0.6.x maintained, free.
design: Two independent checkers over the same code by design (path A from the types discussion): RBS in sig/*.rbs for Steep, inline sig {} for Sorbet. Drift between the two sig sets is EXPECTED AND WELCOME — divergences are food for thought / signal worth examining, NOT bugs to reconcile or DRY away. Accepts what rush-211 originally rejected (runtime dep + code pollution): that trade is the explicit user choice. Reasonableness of the dual-maintenance approach to be evaluated separately by the user.
acceptance_criteria: srb tc green in default rake (separate gate from steep check); lib/ at # typed: true or higher; sig {} on the public API; documented as an independent gate; no attempt to keep Sorbet and RBS sigs in lockstep.

title: Differential harness: close inherited fds (close_others) to stop >&9 flakiness
description: The differential snippets 'true >&9', 'echo x >&9' redirect to an fd never opened in-shell. When the rspec launcher leaks an open fd 9 (e.g. an interactive shell that ran exec 9>...), dash dups the real inherited fd (rc 0) but rush, which only tracks fds it opened, errors (rc 2) — so the test flaked depending on the launcher's fd table. Spawn both subprocesses with close_others: true so the comparison is hermetic and >&9 means 'unopened fd' for both. Not caused by the test/( ) grouping work; that commit only shifted example indices.

title: Expand all endless methods to def...end (honest: splits + tests)
description: User directive: convert all 459 endless defs to def...end for one uniform method form, no nocov shortcuts. Autocorrected the expansion + layout. Fallout fixed honestly: (1) 4 classes split to stay under ClassLength 100 — Executor->TrapRunner (signal/trap subsystem), Lexer->HeredocReader, WordScanner->DollarScanner, SystemCalls file-tests->FileTests mixin; (2) 6 method bodies the endless form had masked from coverage are now tested for real (SystemCalls#times, ClosedStream close/flush, WordSegment ==/hash, Subshell#execute) — no new nocov. Style/EndlessMethod set to disallow to enforce going forward.
close_reason: Done in commit 3fe9848 (refactor: expand all endless methods to def...end); lib now has 0 endless defs, Style/EndlessMethod set to disallow. Bead was left open after the work landed.

title: Harden RuboCop: enable Style/Documentation + opt-in default-off cops
description: NewCops:enable only turns on pending cops, not RuboCop's default-disabled (opt-in) cops. Audited the disabled set; enabled the high-value ones that fit the project's compact style — Style/Documentation (now double-guarded with reek's IrresponsibleModule, by choice), Style/UnlessLogicalOperators (the 'crocodile' unless), Style/InvertibleUnlessCondition, Style/YodaExpression, Style/ImplicitRuntimeError, Lint/ShadowingOuterLocalVariable, Naming/InclusiveLanguage. Fixed the 3 sites the unless/yoda cops flagged.

title: reek: enable curated-off detectors one at a time (non-rubocop), refactoring code to pass
description: Follow-on to rush-211.1. The initial reek ratchet disabled ~13 non-metric detectors with rationales. Re-enable them one detector per slice: genuinely refactor the code to pass real smells, and for true false positives keep the detector ENABLED with a scoped reek 'exclude' (so it still catches new code) instead of a blanket disable. Detectors overlapping RuboCop Metrics (TooMany*, LongParameterList) stay disabled — RuboCop owns those. Order ascending by warning count: ManualDispatch, MissingSafeMethod, ModuleInitialize, BooleanParameter, DataClump, Attribute, IrresponsibleModule, ControlParameter, InstanceVariableAssumption, NilCheck, FeatureEnvy, DuplicateMethodCall, UtilityFunction.
notes: Progress: 9/13 detectors enabled (ManualDispatch, MissingSafeMethod, ModuleInitialize, BooleanParameter, DataClump, Attribute, IrresponsibleModule, ControlParameter, InstanceVariableAssumption). Remaining: NilCheck, FeatureEnvy, DuplicateMethodCall, UtilityFunction.
close_reason: All 13 curated-off non-RuboCop detectors enabled one per slice (35d5abf..59704c8); RuboCop Metrics overlaps left disabled per instruction.

title: reek: add dev dep, curated .reek.yml, wire into rake gate
description: Goal: reek passes with ALL detectors ENABLED, by refactoring the code (not blanket-disabling). Enable detectors one at a time, fixing each; start with the rubocop-overlapping metric detectors (TooManyStatements/Methods/InstanceVariables/Constants, LongParameterList) by reconciling rubocop thresholds, then the design detectors. .reek.yml's disabled-list shrinks to empty over successive slices.
close_reason: reek dev dep + curated .reek.yml + rake gate added in 4e3b143.

title: RBS + Steep: Steepfile + sig/, steep check in rake gate
description: RBS + Steep — ONE of two INDEPENDENT type systems (the other is Sorbet, see sibling bead). Steepfile targeting lib/, sig/*.rbs bootstrapped via typeprof / rbs prototype then tightened gradually, steep check green in default rake. rbs 4.0.3 + typeprof 0.31.1 + Steep 2.0.0 available. NOTE: the earlier 'chosen over Sorbet' rationale is SUPERSEDED — per user directive rush runs BOTH RBS/Steep and Sorbet independently, with intentional, welcome drift between the two formats.
notes: Slice 1 landed (infra + green baseline): steep 2.0.0 + rbs 4.0.3 dev deps, Steepfile (target lib, sig/), sig/ bootstrapped via 'rbs prototype rb' (mostly untyped), 'steep check' wired into default rake gate — green. 108/122 files checked, 14 ignored with per-file reasons in Steepfile, ~54% calls typed at baseline. Ruby::UnannotatedEmptyCollection silenced during bootstrap. Findings recorded in docs/journal.md (### types). REMAINING (not yet done): type the 14 deferred files / remove ignores / tighten sigs — see follow-up beads.
close_reason: Infra deliverable shipped in 335eca4 (Steepfile + sig/ + steep check green in default rake). Ongoing gradual real-typing tracked in rush-211.5.

title: Dev tooling hardening: reek + dual types (RBS/Steep + Sorbet, independent) + mutant
description: Add quality gates beyond rubocop+rspec+100% coverage: reek (done), TWO independent type checkers, and mutant. TYPE DECISION REVISED per user directive: instead of one type system, rush runs RBS/Steep AND Sorbet as two independent gates with two separate formats (sig/*.rbs vs inline sig {}); drift between them is intentional and treated as signal, not error. Verified on Ruby 4.0.5: reek 6.5.0 clean; RBS 4.0.3 + Steep 2.0.0; mutant 0.16.3 free for OSS.

title: subshell trap/var/function isolation hardening
description: Audit and harden ( ) and command-substitution isolation: traps reset to default, var/function/option mutations don't leak to the parent, $$ preserved. Differential + targeted specs.

title: set -C (noclobber) + >| override
description: Under set -C, '>' on an existing regular file fails (no truncation); '>|' forces it. The clobber redirect already parses; add the noclobber stat-check in the file redirect path.

title: set -o pipefail
description: Pipeline exit status becomes the rightmost non-zero stage's status (0 if all succeed). dash 0.5.13.4+ supports it. Wire into PipelineRunner status selection.

title: getopts builtin (OPTARG/OPTIND, silent : mode)
description: getopts optstring name [args]: parse into name, set OPTARG/OPTIND; leading-colon optstring = silent mode (':' for missing arg, '?' for bad opt). Regular builtin. Verify vs dash.

title: shift past $# should abort the shell (special-builtin error)
description: `X=1; shift` with no positionals — dash: shift count exceeds $# is a special-builtin error → abort non-interactive shell with 2 (fires EXIT trap); rush no-ops with status 1. Reuse the BuiltinError fatal mechanism (7ab). Found by the 7aj fuzz; orthogonal. Verify vs dash + corpus.
close_reason: shift now raises BuiltinError (special-builtin abort 2 + EXIT trap) for n>$# and bad operand; operand validated via Base#numeric_operand which matches dash's number(); verified vs dash incl. subshell isolation + corpus + unit; green rake 100% cov

title: Phase 3 — Full POSIX scripting compliance
[finding] Steep 2.0.0 internal crashes on Data.define-with-methods and nested hash destructuring
description: Steep 2.0.0 raises internal errors and SILENTLY swallows them (logs FATAL to stderr, skips the file, still exits 0 'No type error detected') on two patterns rush uses: (1) Data.define blocks that define methods -> 'Unexpected self_type: untyped' (ast/param_ref.rb, expansion/arithmetic/nodes.rb); (2) nested block-param destructuring over a heterogeneously-typed hash -> 'to_ary returns non-array-ish type' (redirection/registry.rb: DEFAULTS.each { |kind, (mode, fd)| }). Impact: crashing files look green but are unchecked. Workaround in place: ignore them in Steepfile. Action: minimal repros + report upstream (soutaro/steep); revisit ignores when fixed. Related rbs-core gap: spawn/exec/fork/exit! declared only on Kernel, not singleton(Process).
acceptance_criteria: Minimal repros captured; upstream issue(s) filed or confirmed existing; note which Steep version fixes each.
notes: Root cause found + param_ref fixed (commit 63d5999). The crash = rbs prototype emits 'Foo: untyped' constant for Data.define-with-block -> self_type untyped -> for_new_method raises. Fix: declare real class (class X < ::Data). param_ref un-ignored, 71% typed, green. Registry crash (nested destructuring) still open. ASYMMETRY: instance methods in a Data.define block attribute to the enclosing module (not the class) so nodes/pipeline_runner still can't be checked without moving methods out of the block into a reopened class (code restructure — needs user decision). Ignore list 6 -> 5.
close_reason: Resolved (commit 34c3645): Data.define crash root-caused (prototype emits 'Foo: untyped' constant -> self_type untyped -> for_new_method raises) and fixed structurally via 'class X < Data.define(:members)' with methods in the class body (param_ref, nodes, pipeline_runner all un-ignored, no crash). registry's nested-destructure crash also fixed (un-nest block param). Disabled Style/DataInheritance (mandates the un-typeable block form). Findings in docs/journal.md.

title: RBS/Steep: type the 14 deferred files, remove Steepfile ignores, tighten sig/
description: Continue rush-211.2's gradual rollout. The Steepfile currently ignores 14 files (prototype-sig errors, Racc coupling, Process gap, Steep crashes) and silences Ruby::UnannotatedEmptyCollection. Work: write real RBS for the deferred files, remove ignores one by one keeping steep green, re-enable the empty-collection diagnostic, and raise the typed-call % above the ~54% baseline. Files: ast/param_ref, expansion/arithmetic/{nodes,number}, pipeline_runner, system_calls, parser_support, closed_stream, redirection/registry, builtins/{test_expr,printf_formatter,set,kill,trap}.
acceptance_criteria: All previously-ignored files (except the racc-generated parser.rb and any blocked by upstream Steep crashes) checked & green; UnannotatedEmptyCollection re-enabled; typed-call % reported and improved.
notes: number.rb un-ignored (commit 45169ca): Style/SymbolProc scope-disabled for it, 2 lambdas made explicit. STEEP IGNORE LIST = 1 — only racc-generated parser.rb (every hand-written file is now checked). Overall typed ~83%. Remaining is pure typed-call% tightening (untyped: dynamic send, AST-holds-AST, unmodeled collaborators) — diminishing returns, 100% typed% not achievable/not a goal.

title: Honour parent-inherited fds in >&n / <&n redirections (dash-compat)
description: rush resolves a redirection's source fd in n>&m / n<&m only against its own IoTable (fds 0/1/2 plus those opened in-shell via exec). An fd >= 3 inherited open from the parent process is invisible, so 'true >&9' with fd 9 inherited-open raises RedirectError -> status 2, where dash dups the real OS fd and succeeds (0). POSIX and dash operate on the actual process fd table. Goal: make >&n / <&n fall back to the real inherited fd when n is not tracked by the IoTable, matching dash.
design: IoTable currently abstracts logical fds -> IO objects and is the boundary for redirects and child-spawn fd setup. To honour inherited fds, when n is absent from the table, probe the real OS fd (e.g. IO.for_fd(n) guarded by a fcntl F_GETFD / rescue Errno::EBADF) and dup from it instead of erroring; a genuinely-closed fd must still error like dash. Open design question: whether raw OS fds should leak through the IoTable abstraction, and how this interacts with subshell/child fd inheritance and close_redirect bookkeeping.
acceptance_criteria: exec 9>/dev/null; true >&9; echo $?  -> 0 (matches dash); an inherited-open fd works as a redirect source/target; a genuinely-closed fd still errors (status 2). Add specs that control fd state explicitly (the differential harness deliberately closes inherited fds per rush-7ue, so cover this with targeted unit/integration specs rather than ambient-fd differential cases).

title: Pin every RuboCop default-off cop explicitly in config
description: Make .rubocop.yml the single source of truth for opt-in cops: list all 63 default-disabled cops we do not use with explicit Enabled: false (compact inline style, grouped by department), alongside the 8 we opt into (Enabled: true). A RuboCop upgrade can no longer silently flip a default, and every off cop is visible in the config rather than hidden in RuboCop's defaults. No functional change — the disabled set is byte-identical, rubocop still 0.

title: Disable Style/EndlessMethod
description: Endless defs are the project's idiom for one-expression methods (459 across the tree). allow_single_line forbade multi-line endless, so a one-expression method too long for 120 cols was forced into def...end — the same kind of method in two forms, visually noisy next to the sea of endless defs. Disabled the cop so they stay uniform.

title: Enable Style/ReturnNil (bare return convention)
description: Examined the 5 default-off cops the user flagged (InlineComment, ReturnNil, StringHashKeys, Send, MissingElse). Per-case evidence: StringHashKeys/Send/MissingElse/InlineComment stay off (string keys are parsed command names; send targets own private methods with no shadowing; MissingElse is a no-op alone). For ReturnNil the user chose the default 'return' style for a uniform bare return; autocorrected the 9 'return nil' sites.

title: test/[ : accept surrounding whitespace in integer operands (dash/bash-compat)
description: POSIX leaves the lexical form of test's integer operands unspecified (the test page defines no integer syntax and never mentions leading/trailing blanks). dash AND bash both accept surrounding whitespace via their C strtol heritage: [ ' 5' -eq 5 ] and [ '5 ' -eq 5 ] succeed. rush's TestExpr#to_int used /\\A[+-]?\\d+\\z/ and rejected them with exit 2. Both behaviours are conformant; matching dash+bash maximises real-script compatibility. Fix: widen the validator to /\\A\\s*[+-]?\\d+\\s*\\z/ (String#to_i already tolerates the padding); underscores (1_000) and hex (0x10) stay rejected, matching dash.
close_reason: Fixed: to_int validator widened to allow surrounding whitespace; differential-verified against dash.

title: test/[ : support recursive ( ) grouping and >4-arg expressions (dash-compat)
description: Rush::Builtins::TestExpr implements only POSIX's flat 0-4 argument algorithm and hard-caps at 4 args (evaluate raises 'too many arguments' for args.size > 4). dash extends this: it recurses through ( ... ) groupings of any length. So [ ( a = a ) ] (5 args) gives exit 0 in dash but exit 2 in rush, and nested [ ( ( ) ) ] also diverges. This is pre-existing (the old SIZES/four? code behaved identically) and POSIX leaves >4 args unspecified, so it is low priority. NOTE: after the recursive-evaluate refactor this is now nearly free to fix — drop the 'args.size > 4' cap and let the !/wrapped? peeling recurse on any length; add differential cases for [ ( a = a ) ], [ ( ( -n x ) ) ], [ ! ( a = b ) ].

title: heredoc does not expand $((...)) arithmetic
description: In a here-document body, $((expr)) is treated as command substitution of (expr), not arithmetic expansion. e.g. cat <<E ... $((1+1)) ... E yields a '1+1: not found' error and empty output where dash gives '2'. HeredocBody#dollar dispatches only $( -> command_sub and $name -> param; it never checks for the second '(' the way Lexer::WordScanner#dollar_paren does. Surfaced (pre-existing) while making word segments polymorphic. Fix: mirror dollar_paren's $(( detection in HeredocBody, producing an ArithSegment. Verify differentially against dash.

title: cd - should change to $OLDPWD (previous directory)
description: rush's cd does not implement the `cd -` special operand: POSIX/dash treat a single `-` as $OLDPWD (change to the previous working directory and print it). Currently rush treats `-` as a literal directory name and fails with 'No such file or directory'. Surfaced while moving the pwd invariant onto ShellState#move_to (which already maintains $OLDPWD). Fix: in Cd, when the operand is exactly '-', resolve it to state.environment.get('OLDPWD') (error if unset), cd there, and echo the new pwd. Verify differentially against dash.

title: mutant: on-demand rake task (OSS usage), not in default gate
description: Add mutant 0.16.3 + mutant-rspec dev deps and a 'rake mutant' task with --usage opensource (free: rush is MIT+public). Kept OUT of the default green gate (slow: reruns the ~60s suite per mutation). Validates that the 100% coverage is meaningful. unparser roundtrip verified clean on all of lib.

title: Phase 4 — OPTIONAL: job control + interactive
description: Job control: bg/fg/jobs/wait, set -m, process groups (:pgroup), Fiddle-based tcsetpgrp, SIGCHLD self-pipe reaping. Interactive: Reline line editing, login/~/.profile/ENV, PS1/PS2. Hardest to cover deterministically; gated and optional per the original plan.

title: special params PPID, LINENO (+ verify PWD/OLDPWD)
description: Add $PPID (parent pid) and $LINENO (current line number). Audit that logical $PWD/$OLDPWD are already correct.

title: umask + ulimit builtins
description: umask [-S] [mode] (octal + symbolic) via a SystemCalls port; ulimit (getrlimit/setrlimit through Process). Verify vs dash where deterministic.

title: CDPATH search in cd
description: cd searches $CDPATH for a non-absolute operand that isn't ./..; echoes the resolved directory when a CDPATH entry is used. Logical PWD/OLDPWD already maintained.

title: set -a (allexport)
description: Every subsequent variable assignment is automatically exported. Flag on ShellState; honored by CommandRunner/assignment persistence.

title: low-value trio: hash / set -v / times
description: Not cleanly differential-testable: hash (path cache; awkward listing format), set -v (verbose, stderr-only), times (CPU times, non-deterministic). Can only check format/structure. Implement last in Phase 2.
close_reason: added hash (explicit name/-r/sorted listing; no auto-cache, documented divergence), times (Process.times, dash format), set -v (v/verbose option + CLI stderr line-echo); verified vs dash where deterministic + unit specs; green rake 100% cov

title: rush >&n / <&n does not honour fds inherited from the parent process
description: Redirect duplication (n>&m, n<&m) resolves the source fd only against rush's own IoTable (0/1/2 plus fds opened in-shell via exec). An fd >= 3 inherited open from the parent is invisible, so 'true >&9' with fd 9 inherited-open errors (status 2) where dash dups the real fd and succeeds (0). POSIX/dash use the real OS fd table. Low priority: scripts rarely rely on inherited high fds, and the differential harness now closes them. Documenting the divergence.
